reforms.description: The Spanish government has introduced reforms to improve vocational training by transferring responsibility for training to regional governments and increasing financial resources available for vocational training. Additionally, new apprenticeship contracts have been implemented to enhance the school-to-work transition for young individuals, allowing for reduced social security contributions and a structured training component.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving vocational training and apprenticeship opportunities enhances the skills of the workforce, which supports economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been put into practice and are currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms significantly enhance vocational training but do not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: Recent reforms have sought to improve vocational training in a number of ways, including: transferring responsibility for training to regional governments and increasing the financial resources available for vocational training.

reforms.description: Spain offers a 15% tax credit for business expenditures on R&D current costs and 30% for capital costs, along with accelerated amortisation for certain assets, to promote R&D investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credits incentivize businesses to invest in R&D, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit scheme is currently in effect and utilized by businesses.
reforms.importance_rationale: The tax incentives are significant but do not represent a fundamental change in the overall tax system.
reforms.source_quote: Spain offers a 15 per cent tax credit over and above the 100 per cent tax deduction received, for business expenditures for R&D current costs, and 30 per cent for capital costs.

reforms.description: The second phase of Spain's Industrial Technology Action Plan (PATI II) aims to enhance the technological capacity of SMEs by improving access to technology and strengthening infrastructure.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing technological capacity and infrastructure for SMEs, the plan supports innovation and competitiveness in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan has been executed and is currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: This phase represents a significant structural change in how technology is accessed and utilized by SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: The second phase of Spain's Industrial Technology Action Plan (PATI II) is currently underway and gives priority to communication and information technologies.
